title: Infosys Knowledge Institute Releases Responsible AI Research for Agentic AI Era
detailed_summary: Infosys Knowledge Institute released research on responsible AI readiness as agentic AI adoption grows. The study surveyed 1,502 business executives and interviewed 40 senior decision-makers across Australia, France, Germany, UK, US, and New Zealand. It found widespread AI-related incidents and a gap between enterprise AI adoption and responsible AI controls, but did not disclose Infosys financial performance, guidance, contracts, or corporate actions.

key_facts.fact: Infosys Knowledge Institute unveiled findings from its report, Responsible Enterprise AI in the Agentic Era.
The report surveyed over 1,500 business executives and interviewed 40 senior decision-makers across Australia, France, Germany, UK, US, and New Zealand.
The findings show that while 78% of companies see RAI as a business growth driver, only 2% have adequate RAI controls in place to safeguard against reputational risk and financial loss.

important_text_spans.text: while 78% of companies see RAI as a business growth driver, only 2% have adequate RAI controls in place to safeguard against reputational risk and financial loss.
important_text_spans.reason: Supports the central finding of the Infosys research report.

important_text_spans.text: 95% of C-suite and director-level executives report AI-related incidents in the past two years.
important_text_spans.section_heading: AI risks are widespread and can be severe
important_text_spans.reason: Shows the reported scale of AI-related incidents in the surveyed enterprises.

search_keywords: Responsible Enterprise AI in the Agentic Era
processing_notes: Marked non-material because the document is a thought-leadership research press release and does not disclose Infosys financial results, guidance, client wins, corporate actions, or other direct operating updates. Generic company boilerplate and safe harbor language were excluded.
